title: Lepturus cylindricus
taxonomy: Lepturus cylindricus is a species of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family or true grasses. It is further classified into the subfamily Chloridoideae, a subfamily of grasses characterized by their small flowers and seed heads. The genus Lepturus is a part of the tribe Eragrostideae, which consists of grasses with various adaptations to arid and semi-arid environments. The species Lepturus cylindricus is native to various regions, including Australia and parts of Asia and Africa. It is a type of grass that grows in a variety of habitats, from grasslands to open woodlands.

intro: Lepturus cylindricus, also known as Asian sprangletop, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, making it beneficial for treating various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Lepturus cylindricus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Lepturus cylindricus is commonly prepared into teas, infusions, or dried powders to treat various ailments, making it a valuable herb in Asian folk medicine.
